channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ladakh's New Tourism Reforms Aim to Boost Ease of Doing Business
item.description: In a bid to simplify the tourism sector in Ladakh, the government has introduced sweeping reforms to encourage ease of doing business. The Lieutenant Governor, VK Saxena, announced that these reforms ...

item.title: Colombia Hosts Global Summit to Phase Out Fossil Fuels Amid Climate Crisis
item.description: More than 50 countries have gathered in Santa Marta, Colombia, for a key conference aimed at transitioning away from fossil fuels. This event, which runs from April 24 to 29, follows the disappointmen...

item.title: Woman found not criminally responsible for killing her infant is granted absolute discharge by Ontario Review Board
item.description: A woman from Kitchener, Ontario, has been granted an absolute discharge by the Ontario Review Board more than five years after she stabbed her eight‑month‑old son to death during a major psychotic epi...

item.title: Australia's eSafety Commission issues warning to gaming platforms over child safety risks
item.description: Australia's eSafety Commissioner has taken action against major gaming platforms like Roblox, Fortnite, and Steam over concerns about child grooming and online radicalisation. These platforms, popular...

item.title: Financial Mismanagement and Infrastructure Neglect at Baitshoki Secondary School
item.description: Baitshoki Secondary School in Itsoseng, North West, faces severe financial and infrastructure challenges. The school owes over R600,000 following defaulted rental agreements for two photocopiers and a...

item.title: Adam Scott discusses acting, Severance, and career highlights in reader interview
item.description: Adam Scott reflects on his career, creative process, and personal experiences in a wide-ranging interview. Discussing his role in Severance, he explains the meticulous work behind the subtle facial tr...

item.title: Marcos inspects 4PH housing project in Palayan City, Nueva Ecija
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. visited Palayan City in Nueva Ecija to inspect the Palayan City Township Project, part of the government's Expanded Pambansang Pabahay Para sa Pilipino (4PH) ...

item.title: Record Voter Turnout in Bengal and Tamil Nadu Praised by Election Commission
item.description: On April 23, 2026, Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar praised the record-breaking voter turnout in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, calling it the highest since India’s independence. West Bengal rec...

item.title: Mater Hospital Aims to Raise Sh150 Million in 2026 Heart Run for Children's Heart Surgeries and Training
item.description: Mater Misericordiae Hospital in Nairobi is planning to raise Sh150 million through its annual Heart Run scheduled for May 23, 2026. The funds will support life-saving heart surgeries for children, out...

item.title: Winnipeg mayor urges federal government to tighten bail laws amid public safety concerns
item.description: Winnipeg Mayor Scott Gillingham is calling on the federal government to reform Canada’s bail system, arguing that current policies are not adequately protecting public safety. During a visit to Ottawa...

item.title: Jewish man assaulted and called 'baby killer' in antisemitic attack in Slough
item.description: A Jewish man, identified as Moshe, has recounted a terrifying encounter in which he was assaulted and called a 'baby killer' during an antisemitic attack in Slough. The incident occurred on Monday whe...

item.title: Potential Supreme Court Changes Following Justice Alito’s Possible Retirement
item.description: Washington is currently speculating over the potential retirement of Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito, 76, a conservative appointed by George W. Bush in 2005. Former President Donald Trump has prais...

item.title: QShelter holds first ‘2004 M00VE Experience’ to encourage fitness and community wellness
item.description: QShelter Limited, together with The Fitness Religion Company, just held their maiden ‘2004 M00VE Experience’ at the 2004 Estate along Lagos Coastal Road. The event na wellness and lifestyle showcase w...

item.title: Melbourne volunteer marks 50 years helping Anzac Day march run smoothly
item.description: David Arden has dedicated over fifty years to volunteering as a marshal for Melbourne's Anzac Day march, starting in 1975 as the Vietnam War ended. He recalls the crowds lining St Kilda Road and World...

item.title: Western Cape politician granted bail in child trafficking and sexual exploitation case
item.description: A Western Cape provincial parliament member has been granted R15,000 bail by the George Magistrate’s Court following his arrest on serious charges, including trafficking in persons, sexual exploitatio...

item.title: Running Pragmata at 1440p on PS5: Performance Insights and Considerations
item.description: Pragmata, Capcom's latest release, runs on the standard PlayStation 5 at a native 1080p resolution, using FSR 1 for 4K output. Interestingly, by starting the game on a PS5 Pro and transferring the clo...

item.title: Samsung employees seek higher profit share amid AI growth
item.description: Samsung employees have reportedly raised concerns over how the company is distributing profits generated from its AI-related products and initiatives. Workers are calling for a fairer share of the fin...

item.title: Woman Kidnapped From Lagos Home, Gunmen Demand N60 Million Ransom
item.description: Early Tuesday morning, Mrs. Olanike Ogundare get kidnapped from her residence at No. 1 Olufemi Ogundare Street, Oke Agaye, Ijedodo, Lagos. According to locals, the gunmen plan the operation well, dem ...

item.title: Newlyweds Navigate Life Between America and Delhi
item.description: This excerpt from Karan Mahajan's novel 'The Complex' explores the life of newlyweds Gita and Sachin, who have migrated to America but find themselves tethered emotionally and socially to Delhi. Gita ...

item.title: English Clubs and Their Paths to European Competition Next Season
item.description: With the Premier League season nearing its conclusion, eight English clubs are currently set to qualify for European competitions, though the final tally could reach ten depending on outcomes in UEFA ...
